message:
fuzz: add toon_decode structure-aware harness for toon_rust roundtrip and corruption modes

Adds a new libfuzzer target `toon_decode`, plus the matching
`[[bin]]` stanza and `toon_rust.workspace = true` dep in
`fuzz/Cargo.toml`. Structure-aware harness that mirrors the
existing `robot_envelope_json` pattern: an `arbitrary`-derived
input type drives both well-formed round-trips and four targeted
corruption modes.

Why toon_decode
---------------
`toon_rust` is the in-repo TOON (text-oriented object notation)
codec used for agent-visible observation streams. Its decoder
takes attacker-reachable strings (pane text snapshots, external
tool output captured into scrollback) and builds structured
values. A panic or unbounded allocation in the decoder would let
a hostile pane-text payload crash or wedge the observation
pipeline. This target hammers the decode path.

Harness shape
-------------
`ToonDecodeCase { value: RawJsonValue, mutation: Mutation }`:

* `RawJsonValue` spans `Null`, `Bool`, `Int`, `Text`, `Array`,
  `Object`, `NestedObject { name, values, fields }`. Array and
  Object leaves cap at `MAX_ITEMS = 6`; strings cap at
  `MAX_STR_LEN = 96`. Bounded per-field allocation keeps
  exec/s healthy.

* `Mutation` selects one of five post-render mutations applied
  to the TOON string before decode:
  - `Clean`               -- round-trip unchanged (oracle: decode
                             must succeed and match original)
  - `AppendBlankLines(u8)` -- append N blank lines (up to 255);
                             the decoder must handle trailing
                             whitespace without panic
  - `DropLastLine`        -- truncate at last newline; decoder
                             must return a typed error, not panic
  - `DuplicateLastLine`   -- duplicate the final line; exercises
                             duplicate-key / duplicate-row paths
  - `AppendGarbage(String)` -- append an arbitrary-bounded string;
                             must fail-safe

Oracle policy: any panic, assertion, or explosive allocation is
a bug; typed `Err` returns on mutated inputs are accepted and do
not fail the harness.

`toon_rust.workspace = true`
----------------------------
Added as a fuzz-crate dep. The codec is already a workspace
member so this just adds the resolved link -- no new transitive
deps picked up at the workspace level.

Relationship to robot_envelope_json
-----------------------------------
Intentionally parallel structure to the `robot_envelope_json`
harness landed in the same push. Both target serde-like decode
paths on attacker-reachable input; both use arbitrary-derived
case generators with `MAX_STR_LEN` / `MAX_ITEMS` caps; both
separate "valid round-trip" from "mutation" modes so minimized
crashes are clearly categorized.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -24,6 +24,7 @@ serde = { version = "1.0.228", features = ["derive"] }
 serde_json.workspace = true
 serde_yaml = "0.9.34"
 rusqlite = { version = "0.38.0", features = ["bundled", "backup", "functions"] }
+toon_rust.workspace = true
 
 [[bin]]
 name = "fts_query"
@@ -114,5 +115,10 @@ name = "termwiz_csi_parser"
 path = "fuzz_targets/termwiz_csi_parser.rs"
 doc = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "toon_decode"
+path = "fuzz_targets/toon_decode.rs"
+doc = false
+
 [profile.release]
 debug = 1
